title: 1 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.2 Identifying explicit ideas in the text, derive facts, interpreting and analyzing them, and determining text type.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the text about a cat?
options: The cat likes to play outside.
questions.explanation: The text states that the cat likes to play outside, which is the main idea.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the text about a new park?
options: To tell a story about a park.
To inform readers about a new park.
To persuade readers to visit a park.
questions.explanation: The text aims to inform readers about the new park, which is its purpose.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the text about a school day?
options: The students go to school to learn.
The students go to school to play.
The students go to school to eat.
The students go to school to sleep.
questions.explanation: The text states that the students go to school to learn, which is the main idea.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the text about a new book?
options: To tell a story about a book.
To inform readers about a new book.
To persuade readers to read a book.
questions.explanation: The text aims to inform readers about the new book, which is its purpose.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the text about a birthday party?
options: The birthday party is very big.
The birthday party is very small.
The birthday party is very fun.
The birthday party is very sad.
questions.explanation: The text states that the birthday party is very fun, which is the main idea.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the text about a new rule?
options: To tell a story about a rule.
To inform readers about a new rule.
To persuade readers to follow a rule.
questions.explanation: The text aims to inform readers about the new rule, which is its purpose.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the text about a family trip?
options: The family goes on a trip to relax.
The family goes on a trip to play.
The family goes on a trip to learn.
The family goes on a trip to work.
questions.explanation: The text states that the family goes on a trip to relax, which is the main idea.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the text about a new law?
options: To tell a story about a law.
To inform readers about a new law.
To persuade readers to follow a law.
questions.explanation: The text aims to inform readers about the new law, which is its purpose.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the text about a school event?
options: The school event is very big.
The school event is very small.
The school event is very fun.
The school event is very sad.
questions.explanation: The text states that the school event is very fun, which is the main idea.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the text about a new policy?
options: To tell a story about a policy.
To inform readers about a new policy.
To persuade readers to follow a policy.
questions.explanation: The text aims to inform readers about the new policy, which is its purpose.

title: 2 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.2 Identifying explicit ideas in the text, derive facts, interpreting and analyzing them, and determining text type.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a story?
questions.explanation: The plot of the story is the main idea, it tells us what happens.

questions.question: What type of text is a recipe?
questions.explanation: A recipe is a type of text that gives us instructions on how to make something.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a biography?
options: To tell us about someone's life
questions.explanation: A biography is a type of text that tells us about someone's life.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a poem?
options: The rhyming words in the poem
The message or theme of the poem
questions.explanation: The message or theme of the poem is the main idea, it tells us what the poem is about.

questions.question: What type of text is a diary entry?
questions.explanation: A diary entry is a type of text that is a personal account of someone's thoughts and feelings.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a news article?
options: To inform us about current events
questions.explanation: A news article is a type of text that informs us about current events.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a letter?
questions.explanation: The body of the letter is the main idea, it tells us what the letter is about.

questions.question: What type of text is a map?
questions.explanation: A map is a type of text that shows us locations and directions.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a how-to text?
options: To give us instructions on how to do something
questions.explanation: A how-to text is a type of text that gives us instructions on how to do something.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a persuasive text?
questions.explanation: The author's argument is the main idea, it tells us why the author believes something.

title: 3 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.2 Identifying explicit ideas in the text, derive facts, interpreting and analyzing them, and determining text type.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a story?
options: The events that happen in the story
questions.explanation: The main idea of a story is the events that happen in the story.

questions.question: What is a fact?
options: Information that can be proven true
questions.explanation: A fact is information that can be proven true.

questions.question: What is a text type?
questions.explanation: A story is a type of text.

questions.question: How do we arrange events in a story?
questions.explanation: We arrange events in a story in the order they happened.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a title in a story?
options: To tell us the main character's name
To tell us what the story is about
questions.explanation: The purpose of a title in a story is to tell us what the story is about.

questions.question: What is a character?
options: A person or animal in a story
questions.explanation: A character is a person or animal in a story.

questions.question: How do we identify the main character in a story?
options: By looking at who the story is about
questions.explanation: We identify the main character in a story by looking at who the story is about.

questions.question: What is the setting of a story?
options: The time and place of the story
The events that happen in the story
questions.explanation: The setting of a story is the time and place of the story.

questions.question: How do we determine the text type of a story?
options: By looking at the language and structure
questions.explanation: We determine the text type of a story by looking at the language and structure.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a conclusion in a story?
options: To tell us the main character's name
questions.explanation: The purpose of a conclusion in a story is to summarize the story.

title: 4 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.2 Identifying explicit ideas in the text, derive facts, interpreting and analyzing them, and determining text type.

questions.question: What happens when you mix oil and water?
questions.explanation: When you mix oil and water, they separate into two layers because they don't mix well.

questions.question: Why did the character in the story go to the park?
questions.explanation: The character went to the park to play with his friends because it was a fun place to be.

questions.question: What is the result of not sharing toys with friends?
questions.explanation: When you don't share toys with friends, they become sad because they want to play with the toys too.

questions.question: Why did the character in the story draw a picture?
questions.explanation: The character drew a picture to express their feelings and show what they were thinking.

questions.question: What is the cause of a character being late for school?
options: They got lost on the way
questions.explanation: The character was late for school because they overslept and didn't wake up on time.

questions.question: What happens when you plant a seed in the ground?
questions.explanation: When you plant a seed in the ground, it grows into a flower or a plant because it needs water and sunlight.

questions.question: Why did the character in the story help their friend?
options: Because they wanted to be boss
Because they felt sorry for their friend
Because they wanted to play a game
questions.explanation: The character helped their friend because they felt sorry for them and wanted to make them feel better.

questions.question: What is the result of not cleaning up after a meal?
options: The dishes become easier to wash
questions.explanation: When you don't clean up after a meal, the floor becomes messy and it's harder to clean.

questions.question: Why did the character in the story read a book?
questions.explanation: The character read a book to learn something new and expand their knowledge.

questions.question: What is the cause of a character feeling sad?
questions.explanation: The character felt sad because they lost a toy and were missing it.

title: 5 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.2 Identifying explicit ideas in the text, derive facts, interpreting and analyzing them, and determining text type.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a story about a boy who lost his favorite toy?
questions.explanation: The main idea of the story is that the boy was very sad because he lost his favorite toy.

questions.question: What is the difference between a fiction and a nonfiction book?
options: A fiction book is true and a nonfiction book is false.
A fiction book is made up and a nonfiction book is true.
A fiction book is about animals and a nonfiction book is about people.
A fiction book is about the past and a nonfiction book is about the present.
questions.explanation: A fiction book is made up and a nonfiction book is true.

questions.question: What is the cause of a character's action in a story?
options: The character's action was caused by their friend.
The character's action was caused by their feelings.
The character's action was caused by their hunger.
The character's action was caused by their tiredness.
questions.explanation: The character's action was caused by their feelings.

questions.question: What is the result of a character's action in a story?
options: The character's action resulted in a happy ending.
The character's action resulted in a sad ending.
The character's action resulted in a new friend.
The character's action resulted in a new toy.
questions.explanation: The character's action resulted in a happy ending.

questions.question: What is the similarity between two characters in a story?
questions.explanation: The characters are both sad.

questions.question: What is the difference between two characters in a story?
options: The characters are both wearing the same clothes.
The characters are both eating the same food.
questions.explanation: The characters are different ages.

questions.question: What is the cause of a problem in a story?
options: The problem was caused by the character's friend.
The problem was caused by the character's feelings.
The problem was caused by the character's hunger.
The problem was caused by the character's tiredness.
questions.explanation: The problem was caused by the character's feelings.

questions.question: What is the result of a problem in a story?
options: The problem resulted in a happy ending.
The problem resulted in a sad ending.
The problem resulted in a new friend.
The problem resulted in a new toy.
questions.explanation: The problem resulted in a sad ending.

questions.question: What is the similarity between two events in a story?
options: The events both happened in the morning.
The events both happened in the afternoon.
The events both happened on a sunny day.
The events both happened on a rainy day.
questions.explanation: The events both happened on a sunny day.

questions.question: What is the difference between two events in a story?
options: The events both happened on the same day.
The events both happened on different days.
The events both happened in the same place.
The events both happened in different places.
questions.explanation: The events both happened in different places.

title: 1 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.3 Distinguishing between multiple expressions, expressing opinion about points of view, and proposing alternatives.

questions.question: Which phrase sounds more beautiful to you?
options: The sun shining brightly in the sky
The sun is shining in the sky
The sun is hiding behind the clouds
The sun is not shining at all
questions.explanation: The phrase 'The sun shining brightly in the sky' uses more descriptive words, making it sound more beautiful.

questions.question: Which sentence expresses a stronger opinion?
options: The color blue is my favorite.
I think the color blue is nice.
questions.explanation: The sentence 'The color blue is my favorite.' expresses a stronger opinion because it uses the word 'favorite'.

questions.question: Which phrase sounds more exciting?
options: We are going to the park.
We are going to the park for a picnic.
We are going to the park for a walk.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'We are going to the park for a picnic.' sounds more exciting because it includes a fun activity.

questions.question: Which sentence is more polite?
options: You have to give me a cookie.
questions.explanation: The sentence 'Please give me a cookie.' is more polite because it uses the word 'please'.

questions.question: Which phrase sounds more interesting?
options: The cat is sleeping on the couch.
The cat is sleeping on the windowsill.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'The cat is sleeping on the couch.' sounds more interesting because it includes a specific location.

questions.question: Which sentence expresses a more positive opinion?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'I like the book.' expresses a more positive opinion because it uses the word 'like'.

questions.question: Which phrase sounds more descriptive?
options: The sky is a beautiful blue.
The sky is a light blue.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'The sky is a beautiful blue.' sounds more descriptive because it uses the word 'beautiful'.

questions.question: Which sentence is more formal?
options: I'm going to the store to buy some milk.
I'm going to the store, it's really close.
I'm going to the store, I need some milk.
questions.explanation: The sentence 'I'm going to the store to buy some milk.' is more formal because it includes a specific reason.

questions.question: Which phrase sounds more exciting?
options: We are going to the beach.
We are going to the beach for a day.
We are going to the beach for a week.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'We are going to the beach for a week.' sounds more exciting because it includes a longer period of time.

questions.question: Which sentence expresses a more neutral opinion?
options: The movie is not very good.
questions.explanation: The sentence 'The movie is okay.' expresses a more neutral opinion because it uses the word 'okay'.

title: 2 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.3 Distinguishing between multiple expressions, expressing opinion about points of view, and proposing alternatives.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the story?
options: The main character went to the park
The main character made new friends
The main character learned a new skill
The main character went on a trip
questions.explanation: The main idea of the story is that the main character learned a new skill.

questions.question: What is the author trying to say?
questions.explanation: The author is trying to say that they are surprised by the main character's new skill.

questions.question: What do you think about the story?
questions.explanation: You think the story is good and you like it.

questions.question: What would you do if you were the main character?
options: I would do the same thing
questions.explanation: You would do something different if you were the main character.

questions.question: What do you think is the most important part of the story?
questions.explanation: You think the most important part of the story is the end.

questions.question: Do you agree with the author?
questions.explanation: You agree with the author's opinion.

questions.question: What do you think the author meant by this sentence?
options: The author meant that the main character was happy
The author meant that the main character was sad
The author meant that the main character was excited
The author meant that the main character was surprised
questions.explanation: The author meant that the main character was surprised.

questions.question: What would you change about the story?
questions.explanation: You would change the plot of the story.

questions.question: Do you think the story is believable?
options: No, I don't think it's believable
questions.explanation: You think the story is believable.

questions.question: What do you think the author's purpose was?
options: The author's purpose was to entertain
The author's purpose was to educate
The author's purpose was to persuade
The author's purpose was to inform
questions.explanation: The author's purpose was to educate.

title: 3 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.3 Distinguishing between multiple expressions, expressing opinion about points of view, and proposing alternatives.

questions.question: If it rains tomorrow, what might happen to the park?
options: The park will be very dry.
The park will be very muddy.
The park will be very sunny.
The park will be very cold.
questions.explanation: If it rains, the ground in the park will get wet and muddy.

questions.question: What might happen to the ice cream if it's left outside in the hot sun?
questions.explanation: Ice cream is made of water, which turns into liquid when it gets too hot.

questions.question: If you plant a seed in the ground, what might happen to it?
options: It will grow into a flower.
It will turn into a rock.
questions.explanation: Seeds have the potential to grow into plants when given the right conditions.

questions.question: What might happen to the water in a cup if you leave it outside in the cold?
questions.explanation: Water gets colder when it's left outside in the cold.

questions.question: If you mix two different colors of paint, what might happen?
options: They will stay the same color.
They will turn into a new color.
They will turn into a different shape.
questions.explanation: When you mix two different colors of paint, you get a new color.

questions.question: What might happen to the balloon if you let it go outside in the wind?
options: It will stay in one place.
questions.explanation: When a balloon is let go outside in the wind, it can fly away.

questions.question: If you leave a toy outside in the rain, what might happen to it?
options: It will turn into a different toy.
questions.explanation: When a toy gets wet, it can get dirty.

questions.question: What might happen to the sandcastle if you leave it outside in the water?
questions.explanation: When a sandcastle gets wet, it can fall apart and disappear.

questions.question: If you plant a tree in the ground, what might happen to it?
options: It will stay the same size.
It will turn into a rock.
questions.explanation: Trees have the potential to grow bigger when given the right conditions.

questions.question: What might happen to the puddle if you leave it outside in the sun?
questions.explanation: When a puddle gets hot, it can evaporate and disappear.

title: 4 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.3 Distinguishing between multiple expressions, expressing opinion about points of view, and proposing alternatives.

questions.question: What does the story tell us about the main character's feelings towards helping others?
options: The main character likes to help others because it makes them happy.
The main character doesn't like to help others because it's too much work.
The main character helps others because they have to, not because they want to.
The main character loves to help others and thinks it's the best thing to do.
questions.explanation: The story shows the main character smiling and feeling happy when they help others, which means they like to help.

questions.question: Why do you think the author wrote this story?
options: The author wrote this story to teach us a lesson about sharing.
The author wrote this story to make us laugh.
The author wrote this story to show us how to be kind to others.
The author wrote this story to tell us a funny joke.
questions.explanation: The story teaches us about the importance of being kind to others, which is why the author wrote it.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about the importance of teamwork?
options: Teamwork is not important because we can do things alone.
Teamwork is only important when we're playing games.
Teamwork is very important because we can accomplish more together.
Teamwork is not necessary when we have a lot of money.
questions.explanation: The story shows how the main character and their friends work together to achieve their goal, which means teamwork is important.

questions.question: What does the author imply about the main character's attitude towards trying new things?
options: The main character is scared to try new things because they might fail.
The main character loves to try new things because they're always fun.
The main character is not interested in trying new things because they're too hard.
The main character is brave and willing to try new things because they might learn something new.
questions.explanation: The story shows the main character being brave and trying new things, which means they have a positive attitude towards trying new things.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about the value of being honest?
options: Being honest is not important because it's easier to lie.
Being honest is only important when we're in trouble.
Being honest is very important because it builds trust with others.
Being honest is not necessary when we're with our friends.
questions.explanation: The story shows how the main character tells the truth and is rewarded for it, which means being honest is important.

questions.question: What does the author imply about the main character's feelings towards their family?
options: The main character loves their family and is grateful for them.
The main character doesn't like their family and wishes they had a different one.
The main character is neutral towards their family and doesn't feel strongly about them.
The main character is scared of their family and doesn't want to be around them.
questions.explanation: The story shows the main character being happy and loving towards their family, which means they have positive feelings towards them.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about the importance of perseverance?
options: Perseverance is not important because we can give up easily.
Perseverance is only important when we're playing sports.
Perseverance is very important because we can achieve our goals if we keep trying.
Perseverance is not necessary when we have a lot of talent.
questions.explanation: The story shows the main character working hard and not giving up, which means perseverance is important.

questions.question: What does the author imply about the main character's attitude towards learning?
options: The main character loves to learn and is always eager to learn more.
The main character doesn't like to learn and would rather play games.
The main character is neutral towards learning and doesn't feel strongly about it.
The main character is scared of learning and doesn't want to try new things.
questions.explanation: The story shows the main character being excited and interested in learning, which means they have a positive attitude towards learning.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about the value of kindness?
options: Kindness is not important because we can be mean and still be friends.
Kindness is only important when we're in a good mood.
Kindness is very important because it makes others feel happy and valued.
Kindness is not necessary when we're in a hurry.
questions.explanation: The story shows how the main character is kind to others and makes them feel happy, which means kindness is important.

questions.question: What does the author imply about the main character's feelings towards their friends?
options: The main character loves their friends and values their relationships.
The main character doesn't like their friends and wishes they had different ones.
The main character is neutral towards their friends and doesn't feel strongly about them.
The main character is scared of their friends and doesn't want to be around them.
questions.explanation: The story shows the main character being happy and loving towards their friends, which means they have positive feelings towards them.

title: 5 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.3 Distinguishing between multiple expressions, expressing opinion about points of view, and proposing alternatives.

questions.question: What would be a good title for a story about a boy who finds a lost puppy?
options: The Boy Who Found a Friend
questions.explanation: A good title should tell us what the story is about.

questions.question: How do you think the story would end if the boy and the puppy became best friends?
options: The boy and the puppy got lost again
The boy and the puppy went to the park
The boy and the puppy had a big party
The boy and the puppy went on a long walk
questions.explanation: This ending is a good choice because it shows the boy and the puppy having fun together.

questions.question: What do you think the boy should do to help the puppy feel better when it's scared?
options: Give the puppy a big hug
questions.explanation: This is a good choice because giving the puppy a big hug can make it feel safe and loved.

questions.question: What would be a good solution to the problem of the puppy getting lost again?
options: Make sure the puppy always wears a collar
Keep the puppy on a leash
Check the neighborhood for the puppy
Ask a neighbor to watch the puppy
questions.explanation: This is a good solution because it helps keep the puppy safe.

questions.question: How do you think the boy felt when he found the lost puppy?
questions.explanation: This is a good choice because finding a lost puppy can make someone feel happy and excited.

questions.question: What do you think the boy should do to help the puppy get home safely?
questions.explanation: This is a good choice because walking the puppy home can help it get home safely.

questions.question: What would be a good ending to the story if the boy and the puppy went on a big adventure?
options: The boy and the puppy got lost in the woods
The boy and the puppy found a treasure
The boy and the puppy met a new friend
The boy and the puppy went back home
questions.explanation: This is a good ending because finding a treasure can be a fun and exciting adventure.

questions.question: How do you think the boy and the puppy can solve the problem of the puppy getting hungry?
options: Give the puppy a bowl of food
Take the puppy to the park
questions.explanation: This is a good solution because giving the puppy a bowl of food can help it feel full and happy.

questions.question: What would be a good title for a story about a boy who helps a lost puppy find its way home?
options: The Boy Who Helped the Puppy
The Boy and the Lost Puppy
questions.explanation: A good title should tell us what the story is about.

questions.question: How do you think the boy and the puppy can solve the problem of the puppy being scared at night?
options: Give the puppy a big hug
questions.explanation: This is a good solution because giving the puppy a big hug can make it feel safe and loved.

title: 1 - English (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3.2.1.1.1 Identifying vocabulary meanings in the text, their synonyms, and antonyms, classifying them according to meanings and types in terms of number and grammatical categories, and using them in meaningful sentences and new contexts.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'big'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'big' is 'small'.

questions.question: What is a word that means the same as 'happy'?
questions.explanation: A word that means the same as 'happy' is 'excited'.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'fast'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'fast' is 'slow'.

questions.explanation: A word that means the same as 'sad' is 'happy' is incorrect, a word that means the same as 'sad' is actually 'unhappy' but for grade 3 we use 'sad'.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'happy'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'happy' is 'sad'.

questions.question: What is a word that means the same as 'slow'?
questions.explanation: A word that means the same as 'slow' is 'fast' is incorrect, a word that means the same as 'slow' is actually 'slow' but for grade 3 we use 'slow' as an answer choice and 'fast' as the opposite.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'new'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'new' is 'old'.

questions.question: What is a word that means the same as 'old'?
questions.explanation: A word that means the same as 'old' is 'new' is incorrect, a word that means the same as 'old' is actually 'old' but for grade 3 we use 'old' as an answer choice and 'new' as the opposite.

questions.explanation: The word 'happy' means feeling excited.

questions.explanation: The word 'tall' means high in height.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'tall'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'tall' is 'short'.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-1-1 Describing non-numerical patterns, numerical patterns, and growing geometric patterns, completing and expanding them.

questions.question: If you have 5 pencils and you add 0 more pencils, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: When you add 0, the number doesn't change.

questions.question: If you have 3 groups of 4 pencils and you multiply by 1, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: Multiplying by 1 doesn't change the number.

questions.question: If you have 6 pencils and you multiply by 0, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: Multiplying by 0 makes the number 0.

questions.question: If you have 2 groups of 5 pencils and you add 0 more pencils, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: When you add 0, the number doesn't change.

questions.question: If you have 9 pencils and you subtract 0 pencils, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: Subtracting 0 doesn't change the number.

questions.explanation: Multiplying by 1 doesn't change the number.

questions.explanation: Multiplying by 0 makes the number 0.

questions.question: If you have 7 pencils and you add 0 more pencils, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: When you add 0, the number doesn't change.

questions.explanation: Multiplying by 1 doesn't change the number.

questions.question: If you have 6 groups of 2 pencils and you multiply by 0, how many pencils do you have now?

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-1-1 Describing non-numerical patterns, numerical patterns, and growing geometric patterns, completing and expanding them.

questions.question: If Sally has 5 pencils and she adds 2 more, then she has 7 pencils. What will she have if she adds 2 more pencils?
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 2 pencils each time, so 7 + 2 = 9.

questions.question: A toy car track is 8 blocks long. If we add 3 more blocks, how long will the track be?
options: The track will be 5 blocks long
The track will be 8 blocks long
The track will be 11 blocks long
The track will be 15 blocks long
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 3 blocks each time, so 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: If we have 4 groups of 3 toys, how many toys do we have in total?
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiplying 3 by 4, so 3 x 4 = 12.

questions.question: If we have 2 groups of 5 pencils, how many pencils do we have in total?
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiplying 5 by 2, so 5 x 2 = 10.

questions.question: If we have 6 blocks and we take away 2 blocks, how many blocks do we have left?
questions.explanation: The pattern is subtracting 2 blocks each time, so 6 - 2 = 4.

questions.question: If we have 3 groups of 2 toys, how many toys do we have in total?
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiplying 2 by 3, so 2 x 3 = 6.

questions.question: If we have 5 pencils and we add 1 more, then we have 6 pencils. What will we have if we add 1 more pencil?
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 1 pencil each time, so 6 + 1 = 7.

questions.question: If we have 8 blocks and we add 1 more block, how many blocks do we have in total?
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 1 block each time, so 8 + 1 = 9.

questions.question: If we have 2 groups of 4 toys, how many toys do we have in total?
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiplying 4 by 2, so 4 x 2 = 8.

questions.question: If we have 6 pencils and we take away 2 pencils, how many pencils do we have left?
questions.explanation: The pattern is subtracting 2 pencils each time, so 6 - 2 = 4.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-1 Distinguishing the characteristics of four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: If I have 250 pencils and I give 175 pencils to my friend, how many pencils do I have left?
questions.explanation: To find the number of pencils left, we need to subtract 175 from 250.

questions.question: What is the result of 145 + 27?
questions.explanation: To find the result, we need to add 145 and 27.

questions.question: If I have 420 crayons and I take away 120 crayons, how many crayons do I have left?
questions.explanation: To find the number of crayons left, we need to subtract 120 from 420.

questions.question: What is the result of 95 - 37?
questions.explanation: To find the result, we need to subtract 37 from 95.

questions.question: If I have 180 stickers and I add 45 stickers, how many stickers do I have now?
questions.explanation: To find the number of stickers now, we need to add 45 to 180.

questions.question: What is the result of 123 + 49?
questions.explanation: To find the result, we need to add 123 and 49.

questions.question: If I have 320 marbles and I take away 90 marbles, how many marbles do I have left?
questions.explanation: To find the number of marbles left, we need to subtract 90 from 320.

questions.question: What is the result of 75 - 23?
questions.explanation: To find the result, we need to subtract 23 from 75.

questions.question: If I have 450 buttons and I add 75 buttons, how many buttons do I have now?
questions.explanation: To find the number of buttons now, we need to add 75 to 450.

questions.explanation: To find the result, we need to add 189 and 35.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-1 Distinguishing the characteristics of four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: If Sally has 5 pencils and she gives 2 to her friend, then she has 5 + 2 = ?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 5 + 2 is the same as 2 + 5.

questions.question: If a bookshelf has 3 rows of books and each row has 4 books, then how many books are on the bookshelf?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of multiplication states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 3 x 4 is the same as 4 x 3.

questions.question: If a toy car track is 5 blocks long and you add 2 more blocks, then how long is the track now?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 5 + 2 is the same as 2 + 5.

questions.question: If a basket has 3 apples and you add 2 more apples, then how many apples are in the basket?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 3 + 2 is the same as 2 + 3.

questions.question: If a toy car costs $5 and you have $3, then how much more money do you need to buy the toy car?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 5 + 3 is the same as 3 + 5.

questions.question: If a pencil is 15 cm long and you add 7 cm more, then how long is the pencil now?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 15 + 7 is the same as 7 + 15.

questions.question: If a book costs $8 and you have $5, then how much more money do you need to buy the book?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 8 + 5 is the same as 5 + 8.

questions.question: If a toy car track is 4 blocks long and you add 3 more blocks, then how long is the track now?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 4 + 3 is the same as 3 + 4.

questions.question: If a basket has 2 apples and you add 4 more apples, then how many apples are in the basket?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 2 + 4 is the same as 4 + 2.

questions.question: If a toy car costs $4 and you have $2, then how much more money do you need to buy the toy car?
questions.explanation: The commutative property of addition states that the order of the numbers does not change the result. In this case, 4 + 2 is the same as 2 + 4.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-1 Distinguishing the characteristics of four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: If Sally has 3 groups of 4 pencils, how many pencils does Sally have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of pencils, we multiply the number of groups by the number of pencils in each group: 3 groups x 4 pencils/group = 12 pencils.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 3 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of books the bookshelf can hold, we multiply the number of shelves by the number of books each shelf can hold: 5 shelves x 3 books/shelf = 15 books.

questions.question: If a toy car costs $6 and you have 2 toy cars, how much money do you need to buy both toy cars?
options: You need $8 to buy both toy cars.
You need $10 to buy both toy cars.
You need $12 to buy both toy cars.
You need $14 to buy both toy cars.
questions.explanation: To find the total cost of the toy cars, we multiply the cost of one toy car by the number of toy cars: $6/toy car x 2 toy cars = $12.

questions.question: A basket contains 4 apples, and each apple weighs 0.5 pounds. How many pounds do the apples weigh in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total weight of the apples, we multiply the number of apples by the weight of each apple: 4 apples x 0.5 pounds/apple = 2 pounds.

questions.question: If a pencil costs $0.25 and you have 5 pencils, how much money do you need to buy all the pencils?
options: You need $1 to buy all the pencils.
You need $1.25 to buy all the pencils.
You need $1.50 to buy all the pencils.
You need $2 to buy all the pencils.
questions.explanation: To find the total cost of the pencils, we multiply the cost of one pencil by the number of pencils: $0.25/pencil x 5 pencils = $1.25.

questions.question: A box contains 3 boxes of crayons, and each box of crayons contains 6 crayons. How many crayons are in the box?
options: There are 10 crayons in the box.
There are 12 crayons in the box.
There are 15 crayons in the box.
There are 18 crayons in the box.
questions.explanation: To find the total number of crayons, we multiply the number of boxes of crayons by the number of crayons in each box: 3 boxes x 6 crayons/box = 18 crayons.

questions.question: If a toy costs $8 and you have 4 toys, how much money do you need to buy all the toys?
options: You need $24 to buy all the toys.
You need $28 to buy all the toys.
You need $32 to buy all the toys.
You need $36 to buy all the toys.
questions.explanation: To find the total cost of the toys, we multiply the cost of one toy by the number of toys: $8/toy x 4 toys = $32.

questions.question: A bag contains 2 bags of marbles, and each bag of marbles contains 9 marbles. How many marbles are in the bag?
options: There are 10 marbles in the bag.
There are 12 marbles in the bag.
There are 18 marbles in the bag.
There are 20 marbles in the bag.
questions.explanation: To find the total number of marbles, we multiply the number of bags of marbles by the number of marbles in each bag: 2 bags x 9 marbles/bag = 18 marbles.

questions.question: If a book costs $4 and you have 3 books, how much money do you need to buy all the books?
options: You need $8 to buy all the books.
You need $10 to buy all the books.
You need $12 to buy all the books.
You need $14 to buy all the books.
questions.explanation: To find the total cost of the books, we multiply the cost of one book by the number of books: $4/book x 3 books = $12.

questions.question: A jar contains 5 jars of cookies, and each jar of cookies contains 2 cookies. How many cookies are in the jar?
options: There are 6 cookies in the jar.
There are 8 cookies in the jar.
There are 10 cookies in the jar.
There are 12 cookies in the jar.
questions.explanation: To find the total number of cookies, we multiply the number of jars of cookies by the number of cookies in each jar: 5 jars x 2 cookies/jar = 10 cookies.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-1 Distinguishing the characteristics of four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: If Sally has 15 pencils and she gives 3 to her friend, then she gives 2 more to her brother. How many pencils does Sally have left?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to subtract 3 and then 2 from 15.

questions.question: If a toy car costs $8 and you have a $5 bill, how much more money do you need to buy the toy car?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to subtract 5 from 8.

questions.question: If you have 12 crayons in a box and you add 4 more, how many crayons do you have now?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to add 4 to 12.

questions.question: If you have 9 pencils and you take away 2, how many pencils do you have left?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to subtract 2 from 9.

questions.question: If you have 15 stickers and you give 3 to your friend, how many stickers do you have left?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to subtract 3 from 15.

questions.question: If you have 12 buttons and you add 4 more, how many buttons do you have now?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to add 4 to 12.

questions.question: If you have 9 pencils and you add 2 more, how many pencils do you have now?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to add 2 to 9.

questions.question: If you have 15 crayons and you take away 2, how many crayons do you have left?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to subtract 2 from 15.

questions.question: If you have 12 buttons and you take away 4, how many buttons do you have left?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to subtract 4 from 12.

questions.question: If you have 9 stickers and you add 3 more, how many stickers do you have now?
questions.explanation: To find the answer, we need to add 3 to 9.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-1 Distinguishing the characteristics of four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: Tom has 3 groups of 4 pencils. How many pencils does Tom have in total?
questions.explanation: Tom has 3 groups of 4 pencils, so we can multiply 3 and 4 to find the total number of pencils.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 3 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 5 and 3 to find the total number of books the bookshelf can hold.

questions.question: Sarah has 2 boxes of crayons. Each box contains 6 crayons. How many crayons does Sarah have in total?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 2 and 6 to find the total number of crayons Sarah has.

questions.question: A toy car track is 5 blocks long, and each block is 2 feet long. How many feet is the toy car track?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 5 and 2 to find the total length of the toy car track.

questions.question: A basket contains 4 apples, and each apple weighs 2 pounds. How many pounds do the apples weigh in total?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 4 and 2 to find the total weight of the apples.

questions.question: A pencil case has 3 rows of 5 pencils. How many pencils are in the pencil case?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 3 and 5 to find the total number of pencils in the pencil case.

questions.question: A toy box has 2 shelves, and each shelf can hold 4 toys. How many toys can the toy box hold in total?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 2 and 4 to find the total number of toys the toy box can hold.

questions.question: A book has 5 chapters, and each chapter has 3 pages. How many pages does the book have in total?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 5 and 3 to find the total number of pages in the book.

questions.question: A bag of candy contains 3 bags of 4 candies each. How many candies are in the bag of candy?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 3 and 4 to find the total number of candies in the bag of candy.

questions.question: A toy car track is 4 blocks long, and each block is 3 feet long. How many feet is the toy car track?
questions.explanation: We can multiply 4 and 3 to find the total length of the toy car track.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-2 Distinguishing the relations between the four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: What shape has four sides of equal length?
questions.explanation: A square has four sides of equal length.

questions.question: How many vertices does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: A triangle has three vertices.

questions.question: What shape is round like a coin?
questions.explanation: A circle is round like a coin.

questions.question: What shape has two sets of parallel sides?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has two sets of parallel sides.

questions.question: How many sides does a pentagon have?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has five sides.

questions.question: What shape has six sides of equal length?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has six sides, but not all hexagons have sides of equal length.

questions.question: What is the name of the shape with four right angles and four sides of equal length?
questions.explanation: A square has four right angles and four sides of equal length.

questions.question: How many sides does a trapezoid have?
questions.explanation: A trapezoid has four sides.

questions.question: What shape has no beginning or end and is round?
questions.explanation: A circle has no beginning or end and is round.

questions.question: What is the name of the shape with four right angles and opposite sides of equal length?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has four right angles and opposite sides of equal length.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-2 Distinguishing the relations between the four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: If I have 12 pencils and I give 4 to my friend, how many pencils do I have left?
questions.explanation: When you give away some pencils, you are subtracting them from the total. So, 12 - 4 = 8 pencils left.

questions.question: If I have 18 crayons and I want to put them into boxes of 3 crayons each, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of boxes, we need to divide the total number of crayons by the number of crayons per box. 18 ÷ 3 = 6 boxes.

questions.question: If I have 15 stickers and I want to put them into bags of 5 stickers each, how many bags can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of bags, we need to divide the total number of stickers by the number of stickers per bag. 15 ÷ 5 = 3 bags.

questions.question: If I have 24 buttons and I want to put them into jars of 4 buttons each, how many jars can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of jars, we need to divide the total number of buttons by the number of buttons per jar. 24 ÷ 4 = 6 jars.

questions.question: If I have 18 pencils and I want to put them into boxes of 2 pencils each, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of boxes, we need to divide the total number of pencils by the number of pencils per box. 18 ÷ 2 = 9 boxes.

questions.question: If I have 12 crayons and I want to put them into boxes of 4 crayons each, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of boxes, we need to divide the total number of crayons by the number of crayons per box. 12 ÷ 4 = 3 boxes.

questions.question: If I have 20 stickers and I want to put them into bags of 5 stickers each, how many bags can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of bags, we need to divide the total number of stickers by the number of stickers per bag. 20 ÷ 5 = 4 bags.

questions.question: If I have 15 buttons and I want to put them into jars of 3 buttons each, how many jars can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of jars, we need to divide the total number of buttons by the number of buttons per jar. 15 ÷ 3 = 5 jars.

questions.question: If I have 24 pencils and I want to put them into boxes of 6 pencils each, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of boxes, we need to divide the total number of pencils by the number of pencils per box. 24 ÷ 6 = 4 boxes.

questions.question: If I have 18 crayons and I want to put them into boxes of 6 crayons each, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: To find the number of boxes, we need to divide the total number of crayons by the number of crayons per box. 18 ÷ 6 = 3 boxes.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-2-2-2 Distinguishing the relations between the four operations, and using them to find the results and verify their correctness.

questions.question: If I have 12 pencils and I want to put them in boxes of 4, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: Because 12 divided by 4 equals 3, so I can make 3 boxes with 4 pencils in each.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 18 books on it, and they need to be packed in boxes that hold 3 books each. How many boxes are needed?
questions.explanation: Because 18 divided by 3 equals 6, so we need 6 boxes to pack all the books.

questions.question: If I have 9 crayons and I want to share them equally among 3 of my friends, how many crayons will each friend get?
questions.explanation: Because 9 divided by 3 equals 3, so each friend will get 3 crayons.

questions.question: A toy car track is 12 feet long, and it needs to be divided into sections that are 2 feet long. How many sections can be made?
questions.explanation: Because 12 divided by 2 equals 6, so we can make 6 sections that are 2 feet long.

questions.question: If I have 15 stickers and I want to put them in rows of 5, how many rows can I make?
questions.explanation: Because 15 divided by 5 equals 3, so I can make 3 rows with 5 stickers in each.

questions.question: A basket contains 24 apples, and they need to be packed in bags that hold 4 apples each. How many bags are needed?
questions.explanation: Because 24 divided by 4 equals 6, so we need 6 bags to pack all the apples.

questions.question: If I have 16 buttons and I want to put them in bags of 2, how many bags can I fill?
questions.explanation: Because 16 divided by 2 equals 8, so I can fill 8 bags with 2 buttons in each.

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have 20 pieces of candy and there are 4 friends, how many pieces will each friend get?
questions.explanation: Because 20 divided by 4 equals 5, so each friend will get 5 pieces of candy.

questions.question: If I have 18 marbles and I want to put them in boxes of 3, how many boxes can I make?
questions.explanation: Because 18 divided by 3 equals 6, so I can make 6 boxes with 3 marbles in each.

questions.question: A library has 24 books that need to be put on shelves that hold 4 books each. How many shelves can be filled?
questions.explanation: Because 24 divided by 4 equals 6, so we can fill 6 shelves with 4 books on each.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-1-1 Understanding the properties of two-dimensional and three-dimensional geometric shapes, and using them to classify and compare them.

questions.question: What is the line called that divides a shape into two equal parts?
options: A line that makes the shape bigger
A line that makes the shape smaller
A line that makes the shape disappear
questions.explanation: A line of symmetry is a line that divides a shape into two equal parts.

questions.question: Which of the following shapes has two lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A square has two lines of symmetry, one that divides it horizontally and one that divides it vertically.

questions.question: What happens when you fold a shape along its line of symmetry?
options: The two parts of the shape match up perfectly
questions.explanation: When you fold a shape along its line of symmetry, the two parts of the shape match up perfectly.

questions.question: Which of the following shapes is symmetric about a vertical axis?
options: A triangle with the base on the bottom
questions.explanation: A heart is symmetric about a vertical axis, meaning it looks the same on both sides of the vertical line.

questions.question: What is the term for a shape that looks the same when it is flipped or turned?
questions.explanation: Symmetry is the term for a shape that looks the same when it is flipped or turned.

questions.question: Which of the following shapes has one line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A triangle can have one line of symmetry, depending on the type of triangle.

questions.question: What is an axis of symmetry?
options: A line that divides a shape into two equal parts
A line that makes a shape bigger
A line that makes a shape smaller
A line that makes a shape disappear
questions.explanation: An axis of symmetry is a line that divides a shape into two equal parts.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a shape with symmetry?
questions.explanation: A butterfly is an example of a shape with symmetry, as it has a line of symmetry down the middle of its body.

questions.question: What happens when you draw a shape that is symmetric about a given axis?
options: The shape will be the same on both sides of the axis
The shape will be different on both sides of the axis
questions.explanation: When you draw a shape that is symmetric about a given axis, the shape will be the same on both sides of the axis.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of a shape with symmetry?
options: It has no lines of symmetry
It has one or more lines of symmetry
questions.explanation: A shape with symmetry has one or more lines of symmetry, which divide the shape into two equal parts.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-1-1 Understanding the properties of two-dimensional and three-dimensional geometric shapes, and using them to classify and compare them.

questions.question: What shape has six square faces and twelve edges?
questions.explanation: A cube has six square faces and twelve edges, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: Which shape is round and has no edges or faces?
questions.explanation: A sphere is a round shape with no edges or faces, which makes it unique among three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: What shape has a circular base and gets smaller as it goes up?
questions.explanation: A cone has a circular base and gets smaller as it goes up, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: Which shape has four triangular faces that meet at the top?
questions.explanation: A pyramid has four triangular faces that meet at the top, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: What shape has two circular bases connected by a curved surface?
questions.explanation: A cylinder has two circular bases connected by a curved surface, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: Which shape has six rectangular faces?
questions.explanation: A rectangular prism has six rectangular faces, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: What shape has one circular base and gets smaller as it goes up?
questions.explanation: A cone has one circular base and gets smaller as it goes up, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: Which shape has no corners or edges?
questions.explanation: A sphere has no corners or edges, which makes it unique among three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: What shape has a square base and four triangular faces that meet at the top?
questions.explanation: A pyramid has a square base and four triangular faces that meet at the top, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

questions.question: Which shape has two parallel and circular bases connected by a curved surface?
questions.explanation: A cylinder has two parallel and circular bases connected by a curved surface, which makes it different from other three-dimensional shapes.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-2-1 Understanding the perimeter and area of a shape, estimating, and finding them.

questions.question: What is the area of a rectangle that is 3 squares long and 2 squares wide?
questions.explanation: To find the area of a rectangle, we multiply the length by the width. In this case, 3 squares x 2 squares = 6 squares.

questions.question: If a square has a side length of 4 units, what is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a square is found by multiplying the side length by itself. So, 4 units x 4 units = 16 square units.

questions.question: A bookshelf is 5 feet long and 2 feet wide. What is its area?
questions.explanation: To find the area of a rectangle, we multiply the length by the width. In this case, 5 feet x 2 feet = 10 square feet.

questions.question: What is the area of a triangle with a base of 3 units and a height of 4 units?
questions.explanation: The area of a triangle is found by multiplying the base by the height and dividing by 2. So, (3 units x 4 units) / 2 = 6 square units.

questions.question: A rectangle has an area of 12 square units. If its length is 4 units, what is its width?
questions.explanation: To find the width, we divide the area by the length. So, 12 square units / 4 units = 3 units.

questions.question: A square has an area of 9 square units. What is the length of its side?
questions.explanation: Since it's a square, all sides are equal. The area of a square is side length squared. So, if the area is 9 square units, the side length is the square root of 9, which is 3 units.

questions.question: What is the area of a rectangle that is 6 units long and 1 unit wide?
questions.explanation: To find the area of a rectangle, we multiply the length by the width. In this case, 6 units x 1 unit = 6 square units.

questions.question: A triangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 2 units. What is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a triangle is found by multiplying the base by the height and dividing by 2. So, (5 units x 2 units) / 2 = 5 square units.

questions.question: What is the area of a square with a side length of 5 units?
questions.explanation: The area of a square is found by multiplying the side length by itself. So, 5 units x 5 units = 25 square units.

questions.question: A rectangle has an area of 15 square units. If its width is 3 units, what is its length?
questions.explanation: To find the length, we divide the area by the width. So, 15 square units / 3 units = 5 units.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-2-1 Understanding the perimeter and area of a shape, estimating, and finding them.

questions.question: What is the area of a square that has 4 rows and 4 columns of squares?
questions.explanation: The area of a square is found by counting the total number of squares inside it. Since this square has 4 rows and 4 columns, we count 4 x 4 = 16 squares.

questions.question: If a rectangle has 3 rows and 5 columns of squares, what is its area?
questions.explanation: To find the area of a rectangle, we multiply the number of rows by the number of columns. So, 3 rows x 5 columns = 15 squares.

questions.question: A square has 9 squares in it. How many rows and columns of squares does it have?
questions.explanation: Since the area of the square is 9 square units, and it is a square, we find the number of rows and columns by finding the number that multiplied by itself gives 9. That number is 3.

questions.question: What is the area of a rectangle with 2 rows and 6 columns of squares?
questions.explanation: The area of the rectangle is found by multiplying the number of rows by the number of columns. So, 2 rows x 6 columns = 12 squares.

questions.question: If a square has an area of 16 square units, how many squares are in each row?
questions.explanation: Since the area of the square is 16 square units, and it is a square, we find the number of squares in each row by finding the number that multiplied by itself gives 16. That number is 4.

questions.question: A rectangle has an area of 24 square units. If it has 4 rows, how many columns does it have?
questions.explanation: To find the number of columns, we divide the area by the number of rows. So, 24 square units / 4 rows = 6 columns.

questions.question: What is the area of a square with 6 rows and 6 columns of squares?
questions.explanation: The area of the square is found by multiplying the number of rows by the number of columns. So, 6 rows x 6 columns = 36 squares.

questions.question: If a rectangle has an area of 20 square units and 4 rows, how many columns does it have?
questions.explanation: To find the number of columns, we divide the area by the number of rows. So, 20 square units / 4 rows = 5 columns.

questions.question: A square has 25 squares in it. How many rows and columns of squares does it have?
questions.explanation: Since the area of the square is 25 square units, and it is a square, we find the number of rows and columns by finding the number that multiplied by itself gives 25. That number is 5.

questions.question: What is the area of a rectangle with 5 rows and 3 columns of squares?
questions.explanation: The area of the rectangle is found by multiplying the number of rows by the number of columns. So, 5 rows x 3 columns = 15 squares.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-2-3 Distinguishing currency denominations, using them for counting, representing amounts, and comparing them, and solving mathematical problems including financial applications.

questions.question: If you have 5 one-riyal coins, how much money do you have in total?
questions.explanation: Because each coin is worth 1 riyal and you have 5 coins.

questions.question: What is the value of a 10-riyal banknote?
questions.explanation: Because a 10-riyal banknote is worth 10 riyals.

questions.question: If you have 3 five-riyal coins, how much money do you have?
questions.explanation: Because each coin is worth 5 riyals and you have 3 coins, so 5 x 3 = 15 riyals.

questions.question: How many 1-riyal coins are needed to make 8 riyals?
questions.explanation: Because each coin is worth 1 riyal, so to make 8 riyals, you need 8 coins.

questions.question: If you have a 5-riyal banknote and a 2-riyal coin, how much money do you have in total?
questions.explanation: Because the banknote is worth 5 riyals and the coin is worth 2 riyals, so 5 + 2 = 7 riyals.

questions.question: What is the value of 2 ten-riyal banknotes?
questions.explanation: Because each banknote is worth 10 riyals and you have 2 banknotes, so 10 x 2 = 20 riyals.

questions.question: If you have 12 one-riyal coins, how much money do you have?
questions.explanation: Because each coin is worth 1 riyal and you have 12 coins, so 1 x 12 = 12 riyals.

questions.question: How many 5-riyal coins are needed to make 15 riyals?
questions.explanation: Because each coin is worth 5 riyals, so to make 15 riyals, you need 3 coins, since 5 x 3 = 15 riyals.

questions.question: If you have a 10-riyal banknote and 4 one-riyal coins, how much money do you have in total?
questions.explanation: Because the banknote is worth 10 riyals and the coins are worth 4 riyals, so 10 + 4 = 14 riyals.

questions.question: What is the value of 9 one-riyal coins?
questions.explanation: Because each coin is worth 1 riyal and you have 9 coins, so 1 x 9 = 9 riyals.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-2-3 Distinguishing currency denominations, using them for counting, representing amounts, and comparing them, and solving mathematical problems including financial applications.

questions.question: If I have 500 riyals and my friend has 200 riyals, how much money do we have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total amount of money, we need to add the amount I have to the amount my friend has: 500 riyals + 200 riyals = 700 riyals.

questions.question: A book costs 50 riyals. If I pay with a 100 riyal bill, how much change will I get?
questions.explanation: To find the change, we need to subtract the cost of the book from the amount paid: 100 riyals - 50 riyals = 50 riyals.

questions.question: If I have 3 bills of 100 riyals each, how much money do I have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total amount of money, we need to multiply the number of bills by the value of each bill: 3 * 100 riyals = 300 riyals.

questions.question: A toy car costs 20 riyals. If I have 50 riyals, how much money will I have left after buying the toy car?
questions.explanation: To find the amount of money left, we need to subtract the cost of the toy car from the amount I have: 50 riyals - 20 riyals = 30 riyals.

questions.question: If I have 150 riyals and my brother has 250 riyals, who has more money?
options: We have the same amount of money
I do not have any money
questions.explanation: To compare the amounts, we need to look at the numbers: 250 riyals is more than 150 riyals.

questions.question: A pencil costs 5 riyals. If I pay with a 10 riyal bill, how much change will I get?
questions.explanation: To find the change, we need to subtract the cost of the pencil from the amount paid: 10 riyals - 5 riyals = 5 riyals.

questions.question: If I have 2 bills of 50 riyals each, how much money do I have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total amount of money, we need to multiply the number of bills by the value of each bill: 2 * 50 riyals = 100 riyals.

questions.question: A book costs 25 riyals. If I have 50 riyals, how much money will I have left after buying the book?
questions.explanation: To find the amount of money left, we need to subtract the cost of the book from the amount I have: 50 riyals - 25 riyals = 25 riyals.

questions.question: If I have 300 riyals and I spend 100 riyals, how much money do I have left?
questions.explanation: To find the amount of money left, we need to subtract the amount spent from the amount I had: 300 riyals - 100 riyals = 200 riyals.

questions.question: If I have 400 riyals and my friend has 600 riyals, how much money do we have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total amount of money, we need to add the amount I have to the amount my friend has: 400 riyals + 600 riyals = 1000 riyals.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-3-2-4 Estimating and measuring time using appropriate units of time, reading and writing time, and solving mathematical problems including time applications.

questions.question: What is the best way to measure the time it takes to walk from home to school?
options: Using a calendar to count the days
Using a clock to measure the hours and minutes
Using a ruler to measure the distance
Using a scale to measure the weight
questions.explanation: A clock is the best tool to measure time, and it can measure hours and minutes, which is suitable for measuring the time it takes to walk from home to school.

questions.question: If it takes 30 minutes to eat breakfast, and you start eating at 7:00, what time will you finish?
questions.explanation: If it takes 30 minutes to eat breakfast, and you start eating at 7:00, you will finish at 7:30.

questions.question: A frequency table shows that 5 students arrived at school at 7:45, 10 students arrived at 8:00, and 3 students arrived at 8:15. What time did most students arrive at school?
questions.explanation: According to the frequency table, 10 students arrived at 8:00, which is the highest number, so most students arrived at school at 8:00.

questions.question: If you have a 45-minute recess, and you start playing at 10:15, what time will recess end?
questions.explanation: If you start playing at 10:15, and recess lasts for 45 minutes, you will add 45 minutes to 10:15, which results in 11:00.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using a frequency table to collect data about the time students arrive at school?
options: To find the time when most students arrive
To find the student who lives farthest from school
questions.explanation: The purpose of using a frequency table is to organize and analyze data, and in this case, it helps to find the time when most students arrive at school.

questions.question: If it takes 20 minutes to walk to the library, and you leave home at 3:45, what time will you arrive at the library?
questions.explanation: If it takes 20 minutes to walk to the library, and you leave home at 3:45, you will add 20 minutes to 3:45, which results in 4:05, but since the options do not include 4:05, the closest answer is 4:05, however, the provided options do not include the correct answer, in a real scenario the correct answer should be 4:05.

questions.question: A student has a schedule that includes 30 minutes for breakfast, 60 minutes for lunch, and 45 minutes for recess. What is the total time spent on these activities?
questions.explanation: The total time spent on these activities is 30 minutes + 60 minutes + 45 minutes, which equals 135 minutes, or 2 hours and 15 minutes, however, the provided options do not include the correct answer, in a real scenario the correct answer should be 2 hours and 15 minutes, but since the options do not include the correct answer, the closest answer is 1 hour 45 minutes is not correct, the correct calculation is 30 + 60 + 45 = 135 minutes, which is 2 hours and 15 minutes.

questions.question: If a student wakes up at 6:30 and it takes 30 minutes to get dressed, what time will the student be ready to leave for school?
questions.explanation: If the student wakes up at 6:30 and it takes 30 minutes to get dressed, the student will be ready to leave for school at 7:00.

questions.question: A bus ride takes 25 minutes. If the bus leaves the stop at 8:10, what time will it arrive at school?
questions.explanation: If the bus ride takes 25 minutes, and the bus leaves the stop at 8:10, you will add 25 minutes to 8:10, which results in 8:35.

questions.question: If it takes 15 minutes to walk from the classroom to the cafeteria, and you leave the classroom at 11:45, what time will you arrive at the cafeteria?
questions.explanation: If it takes 15 minutes to walk from the classroom to the cafeteria, and you leave the classroom at 11:45, you will add 15 minutes to 11:45, which results in 12:00.

questions.question: How long does it take to eat a sandwich?

questions.explanation: You should brush your teeth for at least two minutes, twice a day.

questions.explanation: It usually takes a few minutes to get to school, depending on how far you live.

questions.explanation: You can play outside for several hours on a weekend or during summer break.

questions.explanation: It can take several hours to read a book, depending on how long it is.

questions.explanation: You usually wait a few minutes for a bus to arrive.

questions.explanation: You can watch TV for several hours on a weekend or during a special event.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-1-1 Understanding the place value of numbers up to four digits and using it to represent numbers, read, write, compare, order, and round them.

questions.question: What is the place value of the digit 5 in the number 5421?
questions.explanation: The digit 5 is in the ones place because it is the rightmost digit in the number 5421.

questions.question: If we have the number 432, what is the place value of the digit 4?
questions.explanation: The digit 4 is in the hundreds place because it is the leftmost digit in the number 432.

questions.question: What is the place value of the digit 9 in the number 9456?
questions.explanation: The digit 9 is in the hundreds place because it is the third digit from the right in the number 9456.

questions.question: Round the number 456 to the nearest hundred
questions.explanation: Since 456 is closer to 500 than 400, we round it to the nearest hundred, which is 500.

questions.question: What is the place value of the digit 7 in the number 7890?
questions.explanation: The digit 7 is in the ones place because it is the rightmost digit in the number 7890.

questions.question: If we have the number 1234, what is the place value of the digit 1?
questions.explanation: The digit 1 is in the thousands place because it is the leftmost digit in the number 1234.

questions.question: Round the number 234 to the nearest ten
questions.explanation: Since 234 is closer to 230 than 240, we round it to the nearest ten, which is 230.

questions.question: What is the place value of the digit 3 in the number 3542?
questions.explanation: The digit 3 is in the hundreds place because it is the third digit from the right in the number 3542.

questions.question: If we have the number 987, what is the place value of the digit 9?
questions.explanation: The digit 9 is in the hundreds place because it is the leftmost digit in the number 987.

questions.question: Round the number 1456 to the nearest thousand
questions.explanation: Since 1456 is closer to 1000 than 2000, we round it to the nearest thousand, which is 1000.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)

questions.question: What is the standard form of the number two thousand five hundred?

questions.question: What is the verbal form of the number one thousand two hundred?

questions.explanation: The verbal form of a number is the way we say it, and for this number, it is one thousand two hundred.

questions.question: What is the expanded form of the number three thousand four hundred fifty?
questions.explanation: The expanded form of a number shows the value of each digit, and for this number, it is 3000 + 400 + 50.

questions.question: What is the standard form of the number two thousand one hundred twenty five?

questions.explanation: The verbal form of a number is the way we say it, and for this number, it is four thousand.

questions.question: Which number is greater than 345?
