title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-1-2 Understanding fractions, representing, reading, writing, comparing, and ordering them.

questions.question: A pizza has 8 slices. If 2 slices are eaten, what fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.explanation: Since 2 slices were eaten, 6 slices are left. The fraction of the pizza left is 6 out of 8, which is 6/8.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 12 books. If 4 books are taken out, what fraction of the books are left?
questions.explanation: Since 4 books were taken out, 8 books are left. The fraction of the books left is 8 out of 12, which is 2/3.

questions.question: A toy box has 9 toys. If 3 toys are added, what fraction of the toys is 3?
questions.explanation: Since 3 toys were added, the total number of toys is 9 + 3 = 12. The fraction of the toys that is 3 is 3 out of 12, which is 3/12 or 1/4.

questions.question: A basket has 6 apples. If 2 apples are eaten, what fraction of the apples is left?
questions.explanation: Since 2 apples were eaten, 4 apples are left. The fraction of the apples left is 4 out of 6, which is 2/3.

questions.question: A pencil has 12 parts. If 4 parts are colored, what fraction of the pencil is colored?
questions.explanation: Since 4 parts are colored, the fraction of the pencil that is colored is 4 out of 12, which is 1/3.

questions.question: A cake has 8 slices. If 2 slices are eaten, what fraction of the cake is eaten?
questions.explanation: Since 2 slices are eaten, the fraction of the cake that is eaten is 2 out of 8, which is 1/4.

questions.question: A box has 9 crayons. If 3 crayons are taken out, what fraction of the crayons are left?
questions.explanation: Since 3 crayons are taken out, 6 crayons are left. The fraction of the crayons left is 6 out of 9, which is 2/3.

questions.question: A toy car has 12 wheels. If 4 wheels are missing, what fraction of the wheels are missing?
questions.explanation: Since 4 wheels are missing, the fraction of the wheels that are missing is 4 out of 12, which is 1/3.

questions.question: A book has 8 pages. If 2 pages are turned, what fraction of the pages are turned?
questions.explanation: Since 2 pages are turned, the fraction of the pages that are turned is 2 out of 8, which is 1/4.

questions.question: A bag has 9 marbles. If 3 marbles are added, what fraction of the marbles is 3?
questions.explanation: Since 3 marbles are added, the total number of marbles is 9 + 3 = 12. The fraction of the marbles that is 3 is 3 out of 12, which is 1/4.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-1-2 Understanding fractions, representing, reading, writing, comparing, and ordering them.

questions.question: What is one half written as a fraction?
questions.explanation: One half is written as 1/2, but since that is not an option, we can also say one half.

questions.question: What is three fourths written as a fraction?
questions.explanation: Three fourths is written as 3/4.

questions.question: What is two thirds written as a fraction?
questions.explanation: Two thirds is written as 2/3.

questions.question: What is one quarter written as a fraction?
questions.explanation: One quarter is written as 1/4.

questions.question: What is three sixths written as a fraction in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Three sixths simplifies to one half because both numbers can be divided by 3.

questions.question: What is two sixths written as a fraction in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Two sixths simplifies to one third because both numbers can be divided by 2.

questions.question: What is six twelfths written as a fraction in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Six twelfths simplifies to one half because both numbers can be divided by 6.

questions.question: What is nine twelfths written as a fraction in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Nine twelfths simplifies to three quarters because both numbers can be divided by 3.

questions.question: What is four eighths written as a fraction in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Four eighths simplifies to one half because both numbers can be divided by 4.

questions.question: What is eight twelfths written as a fraction in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Eight twelfths simplifies to two thirds because both numbers can be divided by 4.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 3)

questions.question: What is an equivalent fraction for one half?
questions.explanation: One half is the same as two sixths because if you cut a pizza into six pieces and you eat two of them, it is the same as cutting the pizza into two pieces and eating one of them.

questions.explanation: One sixth is the same as two twelfths because if you have one group of six and you want to make groups of twelve, you will have two groups of twelve.

questions.question: Which fraction is the same as four eighths?
questions.explanation: Four eighths is equal to one half because if you cut a cake into eight pieces and you eat four of them, it is the same as cutting the cake into two pieces and eating one of them.

questions.explanation: Six twelfths is the same as one half because if you cut a pizza into twelve pieces and you eat six of them, it is the same as cutting the pizza into two pieces and eating one of them.

questions.question: Which fraction is equal to three fourths?
questions.explanation: Three fourths is equal to nine twelfths because if you have three groups of four and you want to make groups of twelve, you will have nine groups of twelve.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-1 Finding and estimating the results of addition and subtraction of whole numbers up to three digits, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 147 pencils in his pencil case. His friend gives him 119 more pencils. How many pencils does Tom have now?
options: 266 pencils is incorrect it is actually 247 no, it is 266
questions.explanation: To find the total number of pencils Tom has, we need to add 147 and 119. 147 + 100 = 247, then 247 + 10 = 257, then 257 + 9 = 266.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 250 books on it. If 175 books are removed, how many books are left on the bookshelf?
questions.explanation: To find the number of books left, we need to subtract 175 from 250. 250 - 100 = 150, then 150 - 50 = 100, then 100 - 25 = 75.

questions.question: Sarah has 300 crayons in her box. She gives 127 crayons to her brother. How many crayons does Sarah have left?
questions.explanation: To find the number of crayons Sarah has left, we need to subtract 127 from 300. 300 - 100 = 200, then 200 - 20 = 180, then 180 - 7 = 173.

questions.question: A basket contains 240 apples. If 98 apples are taken out, how many apples are left in the basket?
questions.explanation: To find the number of apples left, we need to subtract 98 from 240. 240 - 100 = 140, then 140 + 2 = 142.

questions.question: Ashley has 180 stickers in her collection. Her mom buys her 122 more stickers. How many stickers does Ashley have now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of stickers Ashley has, we need to add 180 and 122. 180 + 100 = 280, then 280 + 20 = 300, then 300 + 2 = 302.

questions.question: A toy car track is 150 meters long. If it is extended by 127 meters, how long is the track now?
questions.explanation: To find the total length of the track, we need to add 150 and 127. 150 + 100 = 250, then 250 + 20 = 270, then 270 + 7 = 277.

questions.question: Ben has 270 marbles in his bag. He loses 139 marbles. How many marbles does Ben have left?
questions.explanation: To find the number of marbles Ben has left, we need to subtract 139 from 270. 270 - 100 = 170, then 170 - 30 = 140, then 140 - 9 = 131.

questions.question: Emily has 240 buttons in her jar. She gives 168 buttons to her sister. How many buttons does Emily have left?
questions.explanation: To find the number of buttons Emily has left, we need to subtract 168 from 240. 240 - 100 = 140, then 140 - 60 = 80, then 80 - 8 = 72.

questions.question: A library has 300 books on a shelf. If 192 books are added, how many books are on the shelf now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of books on the shelf, we need to add 300 and 192. 300 + 100 = 400, then 400 + 90 = 490, then 490 + 2 = 492.

questions.question: David has 180 pencils in his case. He buys 219 more pencils. How many pencils does David have now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of pencils David has, we need to add 180 and 219. 180 + 200 = 380, then 380 + 10 = 390, then 390 + 9 = 399.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-1 Finding and estimating the results of addition and subtraction of whole numbers up to three digits, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 457 pencils in his pencil case. If he gives 127 pencils to his friend, about how many pencils will Tom have left?
questions.explanation: Tom started with 457 pencils. If he gives 127 away, he will have about 457 - 127 = 330 pencils left, which is about 300 pencils.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 245 books on it. If 117 more books are added, about how many books will the bookshelf have now?
questions.explanation: The bookshelf started with 245 books. If 117 more books are added, it will have about 245 + 117 = 362 books, which is about 350 books.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 945 liters of water. If 378 liters of water are already in the tank, about how much more water can be added?
questions.explanation: The water tank can hold 945 liters. If 378 liters are already in the tank, about 945 - 378 = 567 liters can be added, which is about 600 liters.

questions.question: A bakery has 753 cupcakes to sell. If they sell 219 cupcakes, about how many cupcakes will they have left?
questions.explanation: The bakery started with 753 cupcakes. If they sell 219 cupcakes, they will have about 753 - 219 = 534 cupcakes left, which is about 550 cupcakes.

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some candy. If they have 942 pieces of candy and they want to share it equally among 4 friends, about how many pieces of candy will each friend get?
questions.explanation: If the friends have 942 pieces of candy and they want to share it equally among 4 friends, each friend will get about 942 / 4 = 235.5 pieces, which is about 250 pieces.

questions.question: A library has 817 books on a shelf. If they add 192 more books, about how many books will the shelf have now?
questions.explanation: The library started with 817 books. If they add 192 more books, they will have about 817 + 192 = 1009 books, which is about 1000 books.

questions.question: A boy has 654 marbles in his pocket. If he loses 278 marbles, about how many marbles will he have left?
questions.explanation: The boy started with 654 marbles. If he loses 278 marbles, he will have about 654 - 278 = 376 marbles left, which is about 400 marbles.

questions.question: A store has 983 toys to sell. If they sell 421 toys, about how many toys will they have left?
questions.explanation: The store started with 983 toys. If they sell 421 toys, they will have about 983 - 421 = 562 toys left, which is about 550 toys.

questions.question: A girl has 749 stickers in her sticker book. If she gives 317 stickers to her sister, about how many stickers will she have left?
questions.explanation: The girl started with 749 stickers. If she gives 317 stickers to her sister, she will have about 749 - 317 = 432 stickers left, which is about 400 stickers.

questions.question: A farmer has 936 apples in his orchard. If he picks 482 apples, about how many apples will he have left?
questions.explanation: The farmer started with 936 apples. If he picks 482 apples, he will have about 936 - 482 = 454 apples left, which is about 450 apples.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-1 Finding and estimating the results of addition and subtraction of whole numbers up to three digits, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 147 pencils in his pencil case. He gives 27 pencils to his friend. How many pencils does Tom have left?
questions.explanation: To find out how many pencils Tom has left, we need to subtract the number of pencils he gave away from the total number of pencils he had. 147 - 27 = 120.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 150 books on it. If 35 more books are added, how many books are on the bookshelf now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of books, we need to add the number of new books to the number of books already on the bookshelf. 150 + 35 = 185.

questions.question: Sarah has 95 crayons in her box. She finds 17 more crayons under her desk. How many crayons does Sarah have now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of crayons Sarah has, we need to add the number of crayons she found to the number of crayons she already had. 95 + 17 = 112.

questions.question: A toy car track is 120 inches long. If it is extended by 42 inches, how long is the track now?
questions.explanation: To find the new length of the track, we need to add the length of the extension to the original length. 120 + 42 = 162.

questions.question: Ashley has 189 stickers in her collection. She gives 39 stickers to her brother. How many stickers does Ashley have left?
questions.explanation: To find out how many stickers Ashley has left, we need to subtract the number of stickers she gave away from the total number of stickers she had. 189 - 39 = 150.

questions.question: A water bottle can hold 240 ounces of water. If 75 ounces of water are already in the bottle, how much more water can be added?
questions.explanation: To find out how much more water can be added, we need to subtract the amount of water already in the bottle from the total capacity. 240 - 75 = 165.

questions.question: Ben has 216 marbles in his bag. He loses 48 marbles down a drain. How many marbles does Ben have left?
questions.explanation: To find out how many marbles Ben has left, we need to subtract the number of marbles he lost from the total number of marbles he had. 216 - 48 = 168.

questions.question: Emily has 135 buttons in her jar. She adds 25 more buttons to the jar. How many buttons does Emily have now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of buttons Emily has, we need to add the number of new buttons to the number of buttons she already had. 135 + 25 = 160.

questions.question: A library has 270 books on a shelf. If 90 books are removed from the shelf, how many books are left on the shelf?
questions.explanation: To find out how many books are left, we need to subtract the number of books removed from the total number of books. 270 - 90 = 180.

questions.question: David has 198 pennies in his piggy bank. He adds 22 more pennies to the bank. How many pennies does David have now?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of pennies David has, we need to add the number of new pennies to the number of pennies he already had. 198 + 22 = 220.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-2 Understanding the operations of multiplication and division, representing them using models, forming fact family, finding their results, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: If Sara has 3 groups of 4 pencils, how many pencils does Sara have in total?
questions.explanation: Since Sara has 3 groups of 4 pencils, we can find the total by adding 4 + 4 + 4, which equals 12.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 6 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?
questions.explanation: Since the bookshelf has 5 shelves and each shelf can hold 6 books, we can find the total by adding 6 + 6 + 6 + 6 + 6, which equals 30.

questions.question: If it takes 4 rows of 6 chairs to seat all the students, how many students are there in total?
questions.explanation: Since there are 4 rows of 6 chairs, we can find the total by adding 6 + 6 + 6 + 6, which equals 24.

questions.question: If a toy car track is 3 blocks long and you want to make 5 tracks, how many blocks will you need in total?
questions.explanation: Since each track is 3 blocks long and you want to make 5 tracks, we can find the total by adding 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3, which equals 15.

questions.question: If you have 2 groups of 9 crayons, how many crayons do you have in total?
questions.explanation: Since you have 2 groups of 9 crayons, we can find the total by adding 9 + 9, which equals 18.

questions.question: A farmer has 6 baskets of apples, and each basket contains 8 apples. How many apples does the farmer have in total?
questions.explanation: Since the farmer has 6 baskets and each basket contains 8 apples, we can find the total by adding 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8, which equals 48.

questions.question: If you have 5 boxes of pencils, and each box contains 4 pencils, how many pencils do you have in total?
questions.explanation: Since you have 5 boxes and each box contains 4 pencils, we can find the total by adding 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 + 4, which equals 20.

questions.question: If a book costs $5 and you want to buy 3 books, how much will you need to pay in total?
questions.explanation: Since each book costs $5 and you want to buy 3 books, we can find the total by adding $5 + $5 + $5, which equals $15.

questions.question: If you have 9 groups of 3 stickers, how many stickers do you have in total?
questions.explanation: Since you have 9 groups of 3 stickers, we can find the total by adding 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3, which equals 27.

questions.question: If a toy requires 6 batteries, and you want to buy batteries for 4 toys, how many batteries will you need in total?
questions.explanation: Since each toy requires 6 batteries and you want to buy batteries for 4 toys, we can find the total by adding 6 + 6 + 6 + 6, which equals 24.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-2 Understanding the operations of multiplication and division, representing them using models, forming fact family, finding their results, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: If you have 12 pencils and you want to put them in boxes of 4 pencils each, how many boxes can you make?
questions.explanation: Because 12 divided by 4 equals 3, so we can make 3 boxes with 4 pencils in each box.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 18 books on it, and the books are to be packed in bags of 3 books each. How many bags can be filled?
questions.explanation: Because 18 divided by 3 equals 6, so we can fill 6 bags with 3 books in each bag.

questions.question: If you have 15 crayons and you want to put them in bags of 5 crayons each, how many bags can you make?
questions.explanation: Because 15 divided by 5 equals 3, so we can make 3 bags with 5 crayons in each bag.

questions.question: A toy car track is 24 feet long, and it is to be divided into parts that are 4 feet long each. How many parts can the track be divided into?
options: We can divide it into 4 parts
We can divide it into 5 parts
We can divide it into 6 parts
We can divide it into 8 parts
questions.answer: We can divide it into 6 parts
questions.explanation: Because 24 divided by 4 equals 6, so the track can be divided into 6 parts that are 4 feet long each.

questions.question: If you have 20 buttons and you want to put them in jars of 5 buttons each, how many jars can you fill?
questions.explanation: Because 20 divided by 5 equals 4, so we can fill 4 jars with 5 buttons in each jar.

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have 18 pieces of candy and there are 3 friends, how many pieces of candy will each friend get?
questions.explanation: Because 18 divided by 3 equals 6, so each friend will get 6 pieces of candy.

questions.question: If you have 16 pencils and you want to put them in boxes of 2 pencils each, how many boxes can you make?
questions.explanation: Because 16 divided by 2 equals 8, so we can make 8 boxes with 2 pencils in each box.

questions.question: A library has 24 books to be packed in boxes of 6 books each. How many boxes can be filled?
questions.explanation: Because 24 divided by 6 equals 4, so we can fill 4 boxes with 6 books in each box.

questions.question: If you have 12 toys and you want to put them in bags of 2 toys each, how many bags can you make?
questions.explanation: Because 12 divided by 2 equals 6, so we can make 6 bags with 2 toys in each bag.

questions.question: A group of students want to share some markers equally. If they have 20 markers and there are 5 students, how many markers will each student get?
questions.explanation: Because 20 divided by 5 equals 4, so each student will get 4 markers.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-2 Understanding the operations of multiplication and division, representing them using models, forming fact family, finding their results, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: If I have 3 groups of 4 pencils, how many pencils do I have in total?
questions.explanation: Since we have 3 groups of 4 pencils, we can multiply 3 by 4 to find the total number of pencils, which is 12.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 6 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of books the bookshelf can hold, we multiply the number of shelves by the number of books each shelf can hold, which is 5 x 6 = 30.

questions.question: If I have 9 boxes of crayons, and each box contains 8 crayons, how many crayons do I have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of crayons, we multiply the number of boxes by the number of crayons in each box, which is 9 x 8 = 72.

questions.question: A farmer has 6 baskets of apples, with 5 apples in each basket. How many apples does the farmer have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of apples, we multiply the number of baskets by the number of apples in each basket, which is 6 x 5 = 30.

questions.question: If I have 8 rows of 3 chairs, how many chairs do I have in total?
questions.explanation: Since we have 8 rows of 3 chairs, we can multiply 8 by 3 to find the total number of chairs, which is 24.

questions.question: A toy car track is 4 blocks long, and we want to make 6 tracks. How many blocks will we need in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of blocks needed, we multiply the number of tracks by the number of blocks per track, which is 6 x 4 = 24.

questions.question: If I have 9 groups of 5 pencils, how many pencils do I have in total?
questions.explanation: Since we have 9 groups of 5 pencils, we can multiply 9 by 5 to find the total number of pencils, which is 45.

questions.question: A library has 8 shelves, and each shelf can hold 9 books. How many books can the library hold in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of books the library can hold, we multiply the number of shelves by the number of books each shelf can hold, which is 8 x 9 = 72.

questions.question: If I have 6 boxes of markers, with 8 markers in each box, how many markers do I have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of markers, we multiply the number of boxes by the number of markers in each box, which is 6 x 8 = 48.

questions.question: A bakery has 5 trays of cookies, with 6 cookies on each tray. How many cookies does the bakery have in total?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of cookies, we multiply the number of trays by the number of cookies on each tray, which is 5 x 6 = 30.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-1-2-2 Understanding the operations of multiplication and division, representing them using models, forming fact family, finding their results, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 3 groups of 4 pencils. How many pencils does Tom have in total?
questions.explanation: Tom has 3 groups of 4 pencils, so we multiply 3 x 4 to get 12 pencils.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 6 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?
questions.explanation: The bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 6 books, so we multiply 5 x 6 to get 30 books.

questions.question: If Sara has 12 crayons and she wants to put them into boxes of 4 crayons each, how many boxes can she make?
questions.explanation: Sara has 12 crayons and she wants to put them into boxes of 4 crayons each, so we divide 12 by 4 to get 3 boxes.

questions.question: A bakery is making cookies for a party. They need to package 18 cookies into boxes of 3 cookies each. How many boxes can they make?
questions.explanation: The bakery needs to package 18 cookies into boxes of 3 cookies each, so we divide 18 by 3 to get 6 boxes.

questions.question: Ashley has 9 pencils and she wants to share them equally among 3 of her friends. How many pencils will each friend get?
questions.explanation: Ashley has 9 pencils and she wants to share them equally among 3 friends, so we divide 9 by 3 to get 3 pencils.

questions.question: A toy car track is 12 feet long. If it is divided into sections of 4 feet each, how many sections are there?
questions.explanation: The toy car track is 12 feet long and it is divided into sections of 4 feet each, so we divide 12 by 4 to get 3 sections.

questions.question: A water bottle can hold 12 ounces of water. If 4 ounces of water are already in the bottle, how many more ounces can be added?
questions.explanation: The water bottle can hold 12 ounces of water and 4 ounces are already in the bottle, so we subtract 4 from 12 to get 8 ounces.

questions.question: If it takes 5 apples to make a pie and you have 15 apples, how many pies can you make?
questions.explanation: It takes 5 apples to make a pie and you have 15 apples, so we divide 15 by 5 to get 3 pies.

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have 24 pieces of candy and there are 4 friends, how many pieces of candy will each friend get?
questions.explanation: The friends have 24 pieces of candy and there are 4 friends, so we divide 24 by 4 to get 6 pieces.

questions.question: If a book costs $12 and you have a $4 discount coupon, how much will you pay for the book?
questions.explanation: The book costs $12 and you have a $4 discount coupon, so we subtract 4 from 12 to get $8.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 3)
outcomeText: 3-4-4-1-1 Collecting and organizing data from the environment, representing and reading them using bar graphs and symbols, and interpreting them.

questions.question: What type of graph is used to compare data by using bars of different lengths?
questions.explanation: A bar graph is used to compare data by using bars of different lengths.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using symbols in a graph?
options: To make the graph look pretty
To show the title of the graph
To represent data in a simple way
questions.answer: To represent data in a simple way
questions.explanation: Symbols are used to represent data in a simple way, making it easier to understand the information.

questions.question: What type of bar graph has bars that go up and down?
questions.explanation: A vertical bar graph has bars that go up and down, used to compare data.

questions.question: Why do we use graphs to represent data?
options: To make the data look complicated
To make the data easier to understand
To make the data look pretty
questions.answer: To make the data easier to understand
questions.explanation: Graphs are used to represent data in a way that is easy to understand, making it simpler to compare and analyze the information.

questions.question: What is the title of the graph usually used for?
options: To tell what the graph is about
To make the graph look pretty
questions.answer: To tell what the graph is about
questions.explanation: The title of the graph is usually used to tell what the graph is about, giving a brief description of the information being presented.

questions.question: How can we use a bar graph to compare data?
options: By comparing the lengths of the bars
By counting the number of bars
questions.answer: By comparing the lengths of the bars
questions.explanation: A bar graph can be used to compare data by comparing the lengths of the bars, which represent different values or categories.

questions.question: What type of graph is used to show how many of something there are?
questions.explanation: A bar graph is often used to show how many of something there are, making it easy to compare different categories.

questions.question: Why is it important to label the axes of a graph?
options: So we know what the bars represent
So we can compare the data
So we can count the number of bars
questions.answer: So we know what the bars represent
questions.explanation: Labeling the axes of a graph is important so we know what the bars represent, making it easier to understand the information being presented.

questions.question: What is the purpose of collecting and organizing data?
options: To show how many of something there are
To understand and learn from the information
To count the number of things
questions.answer: To understand and learn from the information
questions.explanation: Collecting and organizing data is done to understand and learn from the information, making it possible to make informed decisions or draw conclusions.

questions.question: How can a symbol be used in a graph?
options: To represent a category of data
To make the graph look pretty
questions.answer: To represent a category of data
questions.explanation: A symbol can be used in a graph to represent a category of data, making it easier to understand and compare the information being presented.

title: 1 - English (Grade 6)
outcomeText: 6.2.1.1.2 Distinguishing the main and sub-ideas of the read text, inferring its facts, interpreting, and analyzing it, and determining its type, purpose, and function.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a news article?
options: To persuade readers to buy a product
To inform readers about current events
To entertain readers with a poem
questions.explanation: The main purpose of a news article is to inform readers about current events, providing them with factual information about what is happening in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a fiction text?
options: It is based on real events and people
It is written in a formal tone
It includes made-up characters and events
It is used to persuade readers to a point of view
questions.explanation: Fiction texts are characterized by made-up characters and events, and are often used to entertain or explore the human experience.

questions.question: What type of text is a biography?
options: A type of fiction that tells a made-up story
A type of poetry that expresses feelings and emotions
A type of nonfiction that tells the story of a person's life
A type of article that provides information on a topic
questions.explanation: A biography is a type of nonfiction text that tells the story of a person's life, providing readers with information about their experiences, achievements, and challenges.

questions.question: What is the function of a headline in a news article?
options: To provide a summary of the main points of the article
To grab the reader's attention and entice them to read the article
To provide background information on the topic
To offer an opinion or perspective on the topic
questions.explanation: The function of a headline is to grab the reader's attention and entice them to read the article, providing a brief and catchy summary of the main topic.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a nonfiction text?
options: A novel about a fictional character
A short story about a fantasy world
questions.explanation: A textbook about science is an example of a nonfiction text, providing readers with factual information and knowledge about a specific subject.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a descriptive paragraph?
options: To persuade the reader to a point of view
To provide a summary of the main points
To tell a story or narrate an event
To create a vivid picture in the reader's mind
questions.explanation: The purpose of a descriptive paragraph is to create a vivid picture in the reader's mind, using sensory details and imagery to describe a person, place, or thing.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a persuasive text?
options: It provides factual information about a topic
It tells a story or narrates an event
It attempts to convince the reader to a point of view
It describes a person, place, or thing in detail
questions.explanation: Persuasive texts are characterized by their attempt to convince the reader to a point of view, using arguments, evidence, and emotional appeals to persuade the reader.

questions.question: What type of text is a diary entry?
options: A type of fiction that tells a made-up story
A type of nonfiction that provides factual information
A type of poetry that expresses feelings and emotions
A type of personal narrative that records a person's thoughts and experiences
questions.explanation: A diary entry is a type of personal narrative that records a person's thoughts and experiences, providing a personal and reflective account of their life.

questions.question: What is the function of a conclusion in a text?
options: To introduce the main topic or idea
To provide evidence or supporting details
To summarize the main points and reiterate the main idea
To raise questions or prompt further discussion
questions.explanation: The function of a conclusion is to summarize the main points and reiterate the main idea, providing a final thought or call to action for the reader.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a fiction text?
options: A biography about a historical figure
A news article about a current event
A novel about a fictional character
A textbook about a scientific topic
questions.explanation: A novel about a fictional character is an example of a fiction text, telling a made-up story that explores the human experience and imagination.

title: 2 - English (Grade 6)
outcomeText: 6.2.1.1.2 Distinguishing the main and sub-ideas of the read text, inferring its facts, interpreting, and analyzing it, and determining its type, purpose, and function.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a news article?
options: To inform readers about current events
To persuade readers to buy a product
questions.explanation: A news article is written to inform readers about what is happening in the world, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: Which type of text is a biography?
options: A type of poetry that tells a story
A fictional story about a person's life
A non-fiction text that describes a person's life
A type of advertisement that promotes a product
questions.explanation: A biography is a non-fiction text that describes a person's life, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a commercial advertisement?
options: To inform readers about a scientific topic
To persuade readers to buy a product
questions.explanation: A commercial advertisement is written to persuade readers to buy a product, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: Which type of text is a press release?
options: A type of poetry that expresses feelings
A fictional story about a company
An official statement released by a company or organization
A type of advertisement that promotes a product
questions.explanation: A press release is an official statement released by a company or organization, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the main idea of a descriptive text?
options: To tell a story about a person's life
To describe a place, person, or thing in detail
To inform readers about current events
To persuade readers to buy a product
questions.explanation: A descriptive text is written to describe a place, person, or thing in detail, making option B the correct answer.

questions.question: Which type of text is a scientific report?
options: A type of poetry that expresses feelings
A fictional story about a scientific topic
A non-fiction text that describes a scientific experiment or process
A type of advertisement that promotes a product
questions.explanation: A scientific report is a non-fiction text that describes a scientific experiment or process, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a guidance text?
options: To inform readers about current events
To provide instructions or advice on how to do something
To persuade readers to buy a product
questions.explanation: A guidance text is written to provide instructions or advice on how to do something, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: Which type of text is a dialogue?
options: A type of poetry that expresses feelings
A non-fiction text that describes a person's life
A conversation between two or more people
A type of advertisement that promotes a product
questions.explanation: A dialogue is a conversation between two or more people, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the main idea of an informational text?
options: To tell a story about a person's life
To describe a place, person, or thing in detail
To inform readers about a particular topic or subject
To persuade readers to buy a product
questions.explanation: An informational text is written to inform readers about a particular topic or subject, making option C the correct answer.

questions.question: Which type of text is a literary topic?
options: A type of poetry that expresses feelings
A non-fiction text that describes a scientific process
A text that explores a theme or idea in literature

questions.question: What do you think the author meant by saying that the main character was very brave in the story?

questions.question: Do you think the article about the importance of recycling provided enough information to convince readers to start recycling?

questions.explanation: In this context, 'quiet' means making little or no noise, which is why it is used to ask someone to be silent in a library.

questions.explanation: The word 'empty' is an antonym for 'full' because it describes something that contains nothing, which is the opposite of something that is full.

questions.question: What does the word 'brave' mean in the sentence 'He is a brave firefighter'?
questions.explanation: In this context, 'brave' means showing courage and willingness to face danger, which is why it is used to describe a firefighter.

questions.explanation: The word 'small' is a synonym for 'little' and can be used in the same context to describe something of a small size.

questions.question: What does the word 'polite' mean in the sentence 'He is a polite person'?
questions.explanation: In this context, 'polite' means showing respect and good manners, which is why it is used to describe someone who is respectful.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 6)
outcomeText: 6-4-2-1-1 Distinguishing growing numerical and geometric patterns, generating and generalizing them, exploring relationships from data tables, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: What comes next in the pattern: 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
questions.explanation: This pattern is a geometric sequence where each term is multiplied by 3 to get the next term.

questions.question: A basketball team scored 15, 30, 60, 120 points in four consecutive games. How many points will they score in the next game?
questions.explanation: The pattern of points scored is obtained by multiplying the previous term by 2, indicating a geometric sequence.

questions.question: In a geometric sequence, the first term is 4 and the common ratio is 3. What is the fifth term of the sequence?
questions.explanation: To find the fifth term, we multiply the first term by the common ratio raised to the power of 4, since the sequence starts from the 0th term.

questions.question: A snail is at the bottom of a 20-foot well. Each day, it climbs 3 feet, but at night, it slips back 2 feet. How many days will it take for the snail to reach the top of the well?
questions.explanation: The snail effectively climbs 1 foot per day. To climb 18 feet, it takes 18 days, and on the 18th day, it will reach the top of the 20-foot well.

questions.question: What is the next number in the pattern: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ?
questions.explanation: This is a geometric sequence where each term is obtained by multiplying the previous term by 2.

questions.question: A city's population is growing in a geometric sequence. If the population was 1000 in 2000, 2000 in 2005, and 4000 in 2010, what will the population be in 2015?
questions.explanation: The population doubles every 5 years, indicating a geometric sequence with a common ratio of 2.

questions.question: A bicycle travels 5 miles in the first hour, 10 miles in the second hour, 20 miles in the third hour, and so on. How many miles will it travel in the fifth hour?
questions.explanation: The distance traveled each hour is doubling, indicating a geometric sequence with a common ratio of 2.

questions.question: A person has $100 in a savings account that earns a 20% interest per year, compounded annually. How much money will the person have after 4 years?
questions.explanation: The interest earned each year is 20% of the current balance, resulting in a geometric sequence.

questions.question: A company's profit is increasing in a geometric sequence. If the profit was $1000 in the first year, $1500 in the second year, and $2250 in the third year, what will the profit be in the fourth year?
questions.explanation: The profit is increasing by a factor of 1.5 each year, indicating a geometric sequence with a common ratio of 1.5.

questions.question: A population of bacteria is growing in a geometric sequence. If there were 100 bacteria initially, 200 after 1 hour, 400 after 2 hours, and 800 after 3 hours, how many bacteria will there be after 5 hours?
questions.explanation: The population of bacteria doubles every hour, indicating a geometric sequence with a common ratio of 2.

questions.question: Tom has 15 pencils in a jar. He adds 3 more pencils every day. How many pencils will Tom have after 5 days?
questions.explanation: Tom starts with 15 pencils and adds 3 more each day for 5 days, so 15 + 3*5 = 30 + 5 = 35 pencils.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 2400 liters of water. If 1200 liters of water are already in the tank, what percentage of the tank is filled?
questions.explanation: To find the percentage, divide the amount of water in the tank by the total capacity and multiply by 100: (1200/2400)*100 = 50%

questions.question: A bakery sells 250 loaves of bread per day. If they make a profit of $0.50 per loaf, how much profit do they make in a day?

questions.question: A car travels 250 miles in 5 hours. How many miles does it travel per hour?

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have 48 pieces of candy and there are 8 friends, how many pieces of candy will each friend get?

questions.explanation: Multiply the length by the width: 10*5 = 50 square meters, but the answer is not in the options, so the correct answer is not provided.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 8 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some money equally. If they have $120 and there are 6 friends, how much money will each friend get?

title: 1 - Math (Grade 6)
